title: Sybase Performance Considerations
text: Sybase Performance Considerations You can use Sybase stored procedures and user options to improve the performance of your application. Uniface implements the following features to improve performance: Fields that are not in the component structure and are not explicitly mentioned in ProcScript, are not selected, fetched, inserted, or updated by the DBMS connector. Sybase does not allow NULL bit fields. Therefore, the SYB connector converts any request to store a NULL bit field to store a 0 (FALSE), so the field is skipped. The connector supports the Uniface stepped hitlist mechanism with a default step size of 10 by using Sybase cursors. Note: Sybase cursors may result in poor performance if multiple sessions have to manage multiple cursors. An alternative is to use the same select option to disable the use of cursors for hit list management. For more information, see Same-Select Mechanism for Hitlist Management . The SYB connector can work with a database that has been configured for chained transactions. For more information, see Transaction Control on Sybase . In addition, you can make use of the following options to influence the performance of your application: To limit the number of returned hits, you can use maxhits parameter with the ProcScript read statement. You can also configure the step size using the step parameter. Use Sybase stored procedures. For more information, see Sybase Stored Procedures . Use the disable proc check option, if possible. Use the step size option to configure the size of each step in the hitlist. Use the tds_packetsize option to control the size of data packets Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
